findings.title: Compound actions START_WAITING_FOR_RESPONSE / RESPONSE_RECEIVED / SESSION_ENDED also mutate state.terminal — issue omits them entirely
findings.description: VERIFIED. In useWorktreeUIState.ts the three compound action cases all read/write state.terminal: START_WAITING_FOR_RESPONSE (L229-240, sets terminal.isActive=true, output/realtimeSnippet=''), RESPONSE_RECEIVED (L242-252, sets terminal.isActive/isThinking=false), SESSION_ENDED (L254-260, resets terminal to initialTerminalState). The issue's '削除' table and the 'Reducer削除の擬似コード' only mention the `terminal` slice field and SET_TERMINAL_* actions. If the implementer simply deletes the `terminal` field from State per the pseudocode, these three reducer cases will fail to compile (they reference state.terminal / initialTerminalState). The issue gives NO instruction on what to do with them. They are also import dependencies: useWorktreeUIState.ts imports `initialTerminalState` (L19). Note these compound actions appear to be currently UNUSED in production code (grep shows action creators startWaitingForResponse/responseReceived/sessionEnded are not invoked anywhere in src/ outside the hook itself), so the cleanest path is to delete them too — but the issue must say so explicitly, and must list the corresponding tests (useWorktreeUIState.test.ts L285-323) as needing deletion.
findings.suggested_issue_edit: Add to Step 2 / 削除 table an explicit sub-item: 'Compound reducer cases that touch terminal — START_WAITING_FOR_RESPONSE / RESPONSE_RECEIVED / SESSION_ENDED (useWorktreeUIState.ts L229-260) and their action creators (startWaitingForResponse/responseReceived/sessionEnded) and the WorktreeUIAction union members — verify they are unused in production (they currently are) and delete them, OR rewrite them to drop the terminal mutation. Also remove the `initialTerminalState` import (L19). Add to 影響を受ける既存テスト: useWorktreeUIState.test.ts L285-323 (START_WAITING_FOR_RESPONSE / RESPONSE_RECEIVED / SESSION_ENDED assertions) must be deleted or rewritten.'

findings.title: fetchCurrentOutput (L501-567) is shared by Mobile AND drives prompt / isSelectionListActive / Auto-Yes / serverPoller state — issue does not decide its fate or split mobile's non-terminal needs
findings.description: VERIFIED. The parent's fetchCurrentOutput does much more than fill state.terminal: it calls actions.showPrompt/clearPrompt (state.prompt, L531-535), setIsSelectionListActive (L538), setLastServerResponseTimestamp (L541), setServerPollerActive (L542), and updates the Auto-Yes state map + stop-reason (L545-560). The target hook useTerminalPanePolling explicitly does NOT own Auto-Yes, lastServerResponseTimestamp, serverPollerActive, or history messages (documented in the hook header L15-18). The Mobile UI consumes ALL of these: MobilePromptSheet uses state.prompt.* (L2059-2068), NavigationButtons render gates on isSelectionListActive (L2030), Auto-Yes mobile toggle relies on autoYesStateMap. So after Step 1, the issue cannot simply 'replace state.terminal.output with the hook' — the parent fetchCurrentOutput must KEEP running for mobile's prompt/selection/Auto-Yes, which means mobile would double-poll (parent fetchCurrentOutput + hook) AND mobile's prompt/selection state would come from the parent while its terminal output comes from the hook (two sources of truth). The issue must explicitly decide: (a) mobile keeps parent-driven prompt/selection/Auto-Yes and only moves terminal output/isActive/isThinking/autoScroll to the hook, OR (b) mobile fully moves to the hook for prompt+selection but Auto-Yes/timestamp stays parent-driven. Option (a) is what TerminalSplitPaneContent does NOT do (PC moved prompt+selection to the hook too) so the reference impl is misleading for mobile.
findings.suggested_issue_edit: Add a new section '### Mobile の state 分割方針' clarifying: the hook owns terminal output/isRunning/isThinking/isSelectionListActive/attaching/autoScroll/prompt; but Auto-Yes state map, lastServerResponseTimestamp, serverPollerActive, and history messages remain parent-driven (per useTerminalPanePolling header). State which of state.prompt and isSelectionListActive mobile will source from the hook vs. the parent. Explicitly note that after migration the parent fetchCurrentOutput STILL runs (for Auto-Yes/timestamp/serverPoller/messages) and that mobile will therefore double-poll /current-output (same accepted tradeoff as PC). If mobile prompt/selection move to the hook, list the corresponding parent state (state.prompt slice, isSelectionListActive useState) that becomes mobile-redundant.

findings.title: Parent polling loop (L1353-1367) and 6 other parent call sites of setTerminal* / state.terminal must be migrated/removed — issue's 変更 table only lists 2 files and omits these
findings.description: VERIFIED. Removing the terminal slice breaks these parent references that the issue's 変更 table (only WorktreeDetailRefactored.tsx generic 'Mobile分岐の受け渡し削除' + WorktreeDetailSubComponents.tsx) does NOT enumerate: (1) polling-interval gate `state.terminal.isActive` (L1356 and dep array L1367) — needs a replacement cadence source after removal; (2) MessageInput isSessionRunning={state.terminal.isActive} (L2044) — needs a new source (mobile hook's terminal.isRunning); (3) handleAutoScrollChange uses actions.setAutoScroll (L809-811) wired to MobileContent autoScroll prop (L2008-2009) — must move to the hook's setAutoScroll; (4) worktreeId-change reset calls actions.setTerminalOutput('','') (L426); (5) CLI-tab change effect calls setTerminalOutput/setTerminalActive/setTerminalThinking (L610-612) — the hook already self-resets on compositeKey change so these become dead; (6) handleKillConfirm calls setTerminalOutput/setTerminalActive/setTerminalThinking (L871-874) — needs the hook's refresh() instead. The issue says 'Mobile分岐の state.terminal.* 受け渡しを削除' which covers only L1986-2008, missing L426, L610-612, L809-811, L871-874, L1356/1367, L2044.
findings.suggested_issue_edit: Expand the 変更 table row for WorktreeDetailRefactored.tsx into an explicit checklist of call sites to migrate/remove: L426 (worktreeId reset), L501-567 fetchCurrentOutput terminal writes (L524/527/528), L610-612 (CLI-tab effect terminal resets), L809-811 handleAutoScrollChange, L871-874 handleKillConfirm, L1356+L1367 polling cadence gate on state.terminal.isActive (define replacement: derive from worktree.sessionStatusByCli[activeCliTab].isRunning or mobile hook isRunning), L2044 MessageInput isSessionRunning. Add an explicit acceptance item: 'parent polling cadence after removal must still switch active/idle based on a defined signal.'

findings.title: Action count is undercounted: SET_AUTO_SCROLL must also be removed but does not match the 'SET_TERMINAL_*' glob
findings.description: VERIFIED. The terminal slice owns 4 actions, not 3: SET_TERMINAL_OUTPUT (L22), SET_TERMINAL_ACTIVE (L23), SET_TERMINAL_THINKING (L24), and SET_AUTO_SCROLL (L25). SET_AUTO_SCROLL mutates state.terminal.autoScroll (reducer L65-69) and has its own action creator setAutoScroll (L409-410) and interface member (L287). The issue consistently writes 'SET_TERMINAL_*' (削除 table, 受入条件, Step 2) which by glob does NOT include SET_AUTO_SCROLL. An implementer grepping for SET_TERMINAL_ would leave SET_AUTO_SCROLL + setAutoScroll dangling, causing a TS error (autoScroll field removed but action still references it) or dead code. autoScroll moves to the hook (useTerminalPanePolling.setAutoScroll) for mobile.
findings.suggested_issue_edit: Replace every 'SET_TERMINAL_*' reference with 'SET_TERMINAL_OUTPUT / SET_TERMINAL_ACTIVE / SET_TERMINAL_THINKING / SET_AUTO_SCROLL (4 actions)'. Add to the action-creator removal list: setTerminalOutput, setTerminalActive, setTerminalThinking, setAutoScroll, and their WorktreeUIActions interface members (useWorktreeUIState.ts L272-274, L287).

findings.title: AC 'grep -r "state.terminal" returns 0' will fail on COMMENTS unless comment cleanup is scoped
findings.description: VERIFIED. `grep -rn "state.terminal" src/ tests/` matches comments that survive code deletion: TerminalSplitPaneContent.tsx L11 + L15 (design-note comments referencing the old mobile shim), WorktreeDetailRefactored.tsx L1436 (renderSplitPane comment 'Mobile path is unchanged: it still consumes state.terminal.*'), and the test file WorktreeDetailRefactored-cli-tab-switching.test.tsx L16 ('splitIndex=0 still reads from state.terminal.*'). The literal AC `grep -r "state.terminal" == 0` cannot pass while these comments exist. Either the AC must scope to code (e.g. exclude comments) or comment cleanup must be listed as an explicit deliverable. The test-file comment is naturally addressed by the R3-010 rewrite, but the two TerminalSplitPaneContent.tsx comments and the WorktreeDetailRefactored.tsx L1436 comment are in files the issue's 変更 table does NOT list.
findings.suggested_issue_edit: Add to the 変更 table: TerminalSplitPaneContent.tsx (remove the obsolete state.terminal references in the header doc-comment L11/L15) and note WorktreeDetailRefactored.tsx L1436 comment must be updated. Reword the AC to: 'state.terminal.* を参照するコード（コメント含む）が0件 — grep -rn "state\.terminal" src/ tests/ で 0 件、もしくはコメント残置がある場合は意図的な履歴コメントとして明記。'

findings.title: state.prompt slice fate is left ambiguous — becomes mobile-only after migration; issue scope should state the decision explicitly
findings.description: VERIFIED / PARTIALLY. PC already moved prompt to the hook (TerminalSplitPaneContent uses hook prompt). After mobile Step 1, if mobile prompt moves to the hook too, the parent's state.prompt slice + showPrompt/clearPrompt/setPromptAnswering actions + SHOW_PROMPT/CLEAR_PROMPT/SET_PROMPT_ANSWERING + PromptState become dead. But the parent fetchCurrentOutput still writes state.prompt (L531-535) and MobilePromptSheet/MobileTabBar read state.prompt (L2055, L2061-2063). The issue's スコープ外 does not mention prompt at all, and the title is only about the terminal slice — yet the migration forces a decision: keep mobile prompt parent-driven (then state.prompt stays, fetchCurrentOutput stays) or move it to the hook (then a second cleanup is implied but explicitly out of this issue). Ambiguity here risks the implementer either over-deleting (breaking MobilePromptSheet) or leaving inconsistent dual state. This is linked to S1-002.
findings.suggested_issue_edit: Add an explicit line to スコープ外 (or to the new Mobile state-split section): 'state.prompt reducer slice の削除は本Issueのスコープ外。Mobile の prompt/MobilePromptSheet は引き続き parent fetchCurrentOutput + state.prompt で駆動する（terminal 出力のみ hook へ移譲）。' — and correspondingly clarify in Step 1 that ONLY terminal output/isActive/isThinking/autoScroll move to the hook on mobile, not prompt.

findings.title: R3-010 test rewrite: the current test drives activeCliTab via the split-0 CLI <select>, and the parent still owns fetchCurrentOutput — the rewrite invariant must be restated for the post-removal world
findings.description: VERIFIED. WorktreeDetailRefactored-cli-tab-switching.test.tsx (header L4-17) tests the invariant 'stale fetch responses for the previous CLI never overwrite the active CLI's state' and currently asserts on terminal-output / thinking-indicator testids fed by state.terminal.* for splitIndex=0 on the PC path (mockIsMobile=false). After the terminal slice is removed, splitIndex=0 on PC is owned by useTerminalPanePolling (already true post-#728 for the split panes), so the assertion source changes. The issue's Step 3 says 'useTerminalPanePolling モックに置換' but the existing test renders the real WorktreeDetailRefactored with real split panes — it does not currently mock useTerminalPanePolling. The issue should state whether R3-010 mocks the hook or asserts on the real hook's rendered output, and confirm the test runs on the PC (mockIsMobile=false) path, not the mobile path. Also the AC 'CLI切替時の poller 再起動シナリオ' should be reconciled with the fact that the hook self-resets on compositeKey change rather than restarting an interval keyed on activeCliTab.
findings.suggested_issue_edit: In Step 3, specify the post-removal invariant precisely: 'splitIndex=0 の terminal 表示は useTerminalPanePolling 由来になる。テストは (a) useTerminalPanePolling をモックして compositeKey 変更時の reset を検証するか、(b) 実 hook + fetch モックで stale-response ガードを検証するか、を明記する。mockIsMobile=false (PC経路) で実行する点と、CLI切替が hook の compositeKey reset を起動する点を記述。'

findings.title: Pseudocode 'After' MobileContent receives worktreeId/cliToolId but cliToolId is not currently a MobileContent prop
findings.description: VERIFIED (minor). The 'After' pseudocode shows `function MobileContent({ worktreeId, cliToolId, ... })` calling the hook. Today MobileContent (WorktreeDetailSubComponents.tsx L893) receives worktreeId but NOT cliToolId — the active CLI is `activeCliTab` held in the parent (passed to MessageInput as cliToolId at L2043). So the implementer must add a cliToolId prop to MobileContentProps and thread activeCliTab into it. Also MobileContent is memo()-wrapped; calling a polling hook inside changes its render behavior (it will re-render on every poll tick), which is fine but worth noting vs. the prior pure-props design. Minor because it is implied, but the prop-threading is a concrete additive change the issue does not list.
findings.suggested_issue_edit: In the 変更 table for WorktreeDetailSubComponents.tsx add: 'MobileContentProps に cliToolId を追加し、親から activeCliTab を渡す。terminalOutput/isTerminalActive/isThinking/autoScroll props は hook 由来に置換し削除。' Note that MobileContent will re-render on each poll tick (acceptable, but document the memo implication).

overall_assessment: The issue correctly identifies the high-level refactor (mobile -> useTerminalPanePolling, delete the terminal reducer slice, rewrite R3-010 test) and the direction is sound. However, as written it would lead to an incomplete/broken implementation if followed literally. Three Must-Fix gaps: (S1-001) the three compound reducer actions START_WAITING_FOR_RESPONSE/RESPONSE_RECEIVED/SESSION_ENDED and the initialTerminalState import also touch the terminal slice and are not addressed — deleting the slice breaks compilation; (S1-002) fetchCurrentOutput drives prompt/isSelectionListActive/Auto-Yes/serverPoller/timestamp which the target hook intentionally does NOT own, so the issue must decide what mobile sources from the hook vs. keeps parent-driven (otherwise two sources of truth or a broken mobile prompt/Auto-Yes); (S1-003) at least seven parent call sites (polling-cadence gate L1356, MessageInput isSessionRunning L2044, handleAutoScrollChange, worktreeId reset L426, CLI-tab effect L610-612, handleKillConfirm L871-874) reference state.terminal / setTerminal* and are not enumerated in the 変更 table — notably the polling loop needs a defined replacement signal. Should-Fix items refine the action count (SET_AUTO_SCROLL is a 4th action outside the SET_TERMINAL_* glob), the grep-based AC (comments in TerminalSplitPaneContent.tsx L11/L15 + WorktreeDetailRefactored.tsx L1436 would fail it), the state.prompt scope decision, and the R3-010 test invariant. All seven PM preliminary findings are independently confirmed; S1-001 (compound actions) and S1-008 (cliToolId prop) are additional gaps the PM list did not call out.
